summary: Fix null pointer exception on user login
task_heading: Fix null pointer exception on user login
description: When a user attempts to log in with an email that has never been registered, the backend throws a NullPointerException instead of returning a 404 or a user-friendly error message. Steps to reproduce: 1) Navigate to /login, 2) Enter an unregistered email address, 3) Submit the form. Expected: a 404 response with message 'User not found'. Actual: 500 Internal Server Error.
acceptanceCriteria: Login with unregistered email returns HTTP 404
objective: Ensure the login API handles unregistered email addresses gracefully without throwing a server error.
expectedOutcome: Login endpoint returns a 404 with a clear user-facing error message instead of a 500 Internal Server Error.
connectionsDbDetails: users table in PostgreSQL auth_db schema; /api/v1/login endpoint
successConditions: POST /login with unregistered email returns HTTP 404
Response body contains message: 'User not found'
No stack trace is exposed in the response
validationRules: Email field must be non-null before DB lookup
DB query must handle zero-result case without raising exception

summary: Add CSV export to task management dashboard
task_heading: Add CSV export to task management dashboard
description: As a project manager, I want to be able to export the full task list as a CSV file so that I can analyse task data in spreadsheet tools such as Excel or Google Sheets. The export should include all columns: task_id, task_heading, description, task_type, user_name, status, jira_id, created_at, and updated_at. The file should be named 'tasks_YYYY-MM-DD.csv' and trigger a browser download.
objective: Enable project managers to export task data to CSV for offline analysis in spreadsheet tools.
expectedOutcome: A downloadable CSV file containing all task fields is generated and delivered to the user's browser.
connectionsDbDetails: tasks table; GET /api/v1/tasks endpoint; browser File API for download trigger
successConditions: Exported CSV contains columns: task_id, task_heading, description, task_type, user_name, status, jira_id, created_at, updated_at
File is named tasks_YYYY-MM-DD.csv with today's date
Browser automatically initiates download without page reload
validationRules: Empty task list must export a CSV with header row only
Special characters in text fields must be properly escaped

summary: Migrate database connection pool to async driver
task_heading: Migrate database connection pool to async driver
description: The current SQLAlchemy setup uses a synchronous connection pool which blocks the event loop under high concurrency. Migrate to asyncpg (for PostgreSQL) or aiosqlite (for SQLite in dev/test) and update all repository functions to use async sessions. Ensure all existing tests pass after migration. This is a prerequisite for the planned horizontal scaling work in Q3.
acceptanceCriteria: All repository functions use async sessions
asyncpg used for PostgreSQL, aiosqlite for SQLite
objective: Remove synchronous DB I/O from the async FastAPI event loop to unblock horizontal scaling in Q3.
expectedOutcome: All DB operations use async drivers; no blocking calls remain in the event loop; all tests pass.
connectionsDbDetails: PostgreSQL via psycopg2 (current) → asyncpg (target); SQLite via sqlite3 (current) → aiosqlite (target); affects all files in core/requirements_pod/db/
successConditions: All repository functions are async and use AsyncSession
pytest suite passes with zero failures after migration
No synchronous DB calls remain in the event loop under load testing
validationRules: asyncpg must be used only when DATABASE_URL starts with postgresql://
aiosqlite must be used only when DATABASE_URL starts with sqlite://
Connection pool size must remain configurable via environment variable
